texto: One grey hair appeared on my headI plucked it out with my hand.It answered me: "You have prevailed against me alone -What will you do when my army comes after me?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Yehuda HaLevi que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “One grey hair appeared on my headI plucked it out with my hand.It answered me: "You have prevaile…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, age, aging, com foco em aging e humour.

frase_original: One grey hair appeared on my headI plucked it out with my hand.It answered me: "You have prevailed against me alone -What will you do when my army comes after me?

texto: My heart in the East But the rest of me far in the West— How can I savor this life, even taste what I eat? How, in the bonds of the Moor, Zion chained to the Cross, Can I do what I’ve vowed to and must? Gladly I’d leave All the best of grand Spain For one glimpse of the ruined Shrine’s dust.

How, in the bonds of the Moor, Zion chained to the Cross, Can I do what I’ve vowed to and must? Gladly I’d leave All the best of grand Spain For one glimpse of the ruined Shrine’s dust.
